Title: Mediterranean Tomato Landraces Exhibit Genotype-Specific Transcriptomic Responses to Water Stress.
Summary: This paper investigates the transcriptomic responses of two drought-tolerant Mediterranean tomato landraces and two commercial varieties under well-watered and water-stress conditions. It highlights genotype-specific responses, with landraces showing targeted transcriptional responses to drought, while commercial varieties exhibit a broader metabolic response.
Relevance_Explanation: The paper directly addresses the query by studying the transcriptomic responses of tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) to water stress, which is a form of drought stress. It uses RNA sequencing to analyze gene expression in leaves and roots under different water conditions.
Strain_Variety: de Ramellet; Lucariello; Moneymaker; Ailsa Craig
Sample_Size: 3 biological replicates per genotype and treatment
